name: (c) per-hole match GROSS — subject B wins + open book. Madden beats Josh 4 holes to 2 (2 up); Josh's backer Kyle pays Madden's backer Steven 2 × stake.
_contract: Golden HAND-CALC fixture for per_hole_match (engine/bets/). Proves THREE things at once: (1) GROSS basis — the engine is basis-agnostic, it compares whatever per-hole values it's fed (here gross strokes), so `netPerHoleBySubject` holds gross; (2) subject B wins (negative A-margin) → edge direction flips correctly; (3) the OPEN BOOK (FR8/FR10/FR38) — the SettlementEdge is between STAKEHOLDERS (Kyle, Steven), NOT the subjects (Josh, Madden); a non-playing backer collects. Engine consumes per-hole values as a GIVEN (P2). `expected` is hand-calculated truth. Edge direction: fromPlayerId PAYS toPlayerId. Cents.
_handCalc: Grade each hole on gross, lower wins. Holes 1–2: Josh 3 vs Madden 4 → Josh wins (2). Holes 3–6: Josh 4 vs Madden 3 → Madden wins (4). Holes 7–18: 4 vs 4 → push (12). Josh wins 2, Madden wins 4 → Madden is 4 − 2 = 2 up, so subject B wins by a hole-margin of 2. Money = 2 × $10.00 = $20.00 = 2000c. The money is between STAKEHOLDERS, not subjects: side A's backer is Kyle (backing Josh, the loser), side B's is Steven (backing Madden, the winner). Loser's stakeholder Kyle pays winner's stakeholder Steven. Edge: kyle → steven, 2000c. Kyle/Steven need not have teed off (FR38).
